'''Kian McDermott''' (born 14 February 2006) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>[https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-duo-complete-loan-moves-ahead-of-debut-senior-campaigns-4957231 Wigan Warriors duo complete loan moves ahead of debut senior campaigns]</ref>

==Background==
McDermott was born in [[Wigan, Greater Manchester]], England. He studied at Wigan and Leigh College.<ref>[https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/kian-mcdermott-meet-the-wigan-warriors-rising-prop-living-the-dream-with-best-job-in-the-world-4953249 Kian McDermott: Meet the Wigan Warriors rising prop living the dream with ‘best job in the world’]</ref>

He played for [[Wigan St Patricks]] as a junior. He played for [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]] in the [[Rugby League War of the Roses|Origin series]].<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-warriors-coach-hails-homegrown-teenage-star-with-bright-future-tipped Wigan Warriors coach hails homegrown teenage star with bright future tipped]</ref> He was called up to the England Academy squad in 2023.<ref>[https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61903/england-academy-call-ups-for-wakefield-and-wigan-forwards England Academy call ups for Wakefield and Wigan forwards]</ref>

==Career==
McDermott the made his professional debut in January 2025 on loan at the [[London Broncos]] against the [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[2025 Challenge Cup]].<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-wolves-duo-make-championship-switch-ahead-of-new-season Warrington Wolves duo make Championship switch ahead of new season]</ref>

In May 2025 he made his [[Wigan Warriors]] debut in the [[Super League]] against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>[https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/rugby-league-news-wigan-warriors-debutant-outlines-2025-goals-and-playing-for-his-hometown-club-105005/ Wigan Warriors forwards outlines 2025 goals after ‘shock’ debut]</ref>
